gate_status.detail: Legal play through fortress construction has public replay evidence.
The first persistent-memory design underperformed its memory-off control.
The best unseen-map run reached 4/5 and still missed its second functional room.
Attempts 1 through 27 contain no full-year self-sufficient pass.

findings.title: Real gameplay and successful gameplay are different achievements.
findings.claim: Agents now dig, build, furnish, farm, handle dialogs, and change a live fortress through legal governed actions. The strongest long runs still fail sustainability, structure, or evidence gates.
findings.evidence: Attempt 27 made three functional rooms, two workshops, furniture, and a planted subterranean farm. It remained a policy-diagnostic fail after citizenship evidence was contaminated and resource flow missed the G7 bar.
findings.caveat: A high scalar score or visible construction does not establish a gate pass.

findings.title: Spatial perception changed what models could build.
findings.claim: Text-only policies rarely maintained the hollow-ring invariant needed for rooms. Two of three vision arms closed a ring on their first run after a rendered minimap entered the declared observation.
findings.evidence: The first G4 pass later completed two enclosed functional rooms with 100 recorded screen frames and governed provenance on every step.
findings.caveat: Vision removed a modality barrier; it did not make every model or repeat reliable.

findings.title: The first persistent memory made the policy worse.
findings.claim: A five-run memory-on series declined while the matched memory-off control produced more functional rooms. Failure tallies and a persistent plan amplified wall spam instead of learning.
findings.evidence: Memory-on rubric fell from 90.4 to 82.6; three of five memory-off runs built a functional room while the memory-on arm built none.
findings.caveat: This rejects one memory design, not memory as a capability.

findings.title: The scalar found shortcuts before the fortress did.
findings.claim: Queue spam and later chair-factory monoculture drove large score gains without broader fortress quality. Each exploit forced a versioned scoring boundary and replay audit.
findings.evidence: The 250-step endurance probe compounded economically while remaining at one functional room, directly motivating score-v3.
findings.caveat: Scores from different score versions, seeds, or horizons are not one ranking.

findings.title: Longer runs reveal where a plan stops adapting.
findings.claim: After reaching passing-level structure, the endurance policy stopped expanding and optimized repeated production. More turns exposed a durable strategy failure that a short run hid.
findings.evidence: From step 100 to 250 the score grew 5.35x and goods diversity reached five types, while functional rooms stayed fixed at one.
findings.caveat: The production was real and legal; the failure was strategic degeneration.

findings.title: A new map exposed assumptions the home embark concealed.
findings.claim: The unseen region surfaced wood blindness, drowning risk, dead metrics, and hardcoded terrain assumptions. With the corrected observation stack, GLM-5V advanced from zero rooms to one; the GPT-5.5 escalation reached 4/5.
findings.evidence: Corrected-instrument GLM-5V built its first region3 room. GPT-5.5 vision held population 7/7, built 74 constructions and one functional room, and missed only the second-room criterion.
findings.caveat: G6 remains unpassed. One near-pass does not establish general play.

findings.title: A benchmark can only be as truthful as its observation and evidence.
findings.claim: Phantom objectives, invisible legality walls, queued walls, off-frame proof windows, wood blindness, dead metrics, phantom stock counts, and dropped fields each changed what a run appeared to prove.
findings.evidence: The project repeatedly stopped, invalidated claims, versioned the boundary, and reran rather than converting a measurement defect into model progress.
findings.caveat: Observer Map and derived inspection views remain spectator evidence unless a protocol explicitly gives them to the agent.

evidence_runs.label: Endurance probe
evidence_runs.note: Real production compounded while structure stalled.

evidence_runs.label: Best unseen-map run
evidence_runs.note: Missed only the second functional room.

evidence_runs.label: G7 attempt 1
evidence_runs.note: A real half-year fort that missed survival and structure.

evidence_runs.label: G7 attempt 27
evidence_runs.note: Larger fort; evidence contamination stopped the run.
